our_open_deliverables.text: Invite Elie Sauma and Harsh Sejpal to the Boston roadshow event (late April 2026) featuring Hackett Group on AI and procurement.
Set up a SharePoint site populated with white papers, mini demos, and relevant materials for Sitecore to access and distribute internally.
Assign the same demo resource (Devika) used by the relevant stakeholder to maintain continuity and leverage the trusted reference relationship
Send Sitecore the standard Zycus NDA for review and signature
Provide RFP response and vendor question answers by 30 June 2026 EOD
Send case studies and tax-integration validation info promised in the demo to Ansen.

buyer_dependent_items.text: Sitecore to complete vendor evaluation of the last remaining vendor (week of 15 May) and down-select to 4 finalists by end of that week
Sitecore to provide Zycus with the RFP instructions outlining demo focus areas (vendor onboarding, OFAC, AP workflows) for follow-up demonstrations
Provide RFP FAQ / consolidated vendor question answers to guide the response.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the incumbent being actively displaced (negative buyer sentiment, post-Thoma Bravo decline) and remains the reference point for budget and fit. Zycus is a confirmed finalist of four with a trusted reference and a value-over-price selection bias in its favour. The single largest competitive blind spot is the three other unnamed RFP finalists, whose positioning and pricing are unknown and will be scored against Zycus when responses are due 30 Jun 2026.

competitors.name: Coupa (incumbent)
competitors.quote: Invoice smashed has just not worked for for us as a company… Coupa themselves admitted it's not working, and yet they would not give us credit back… once they got acquired by Tomo Bravo, it just… they've been difficult for the last two, 3 years. (Elie Sauma, 9 April 2026)
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 9 April 2026, Elie Sauma
competitors.how_we_win: Coupa is being replaced, not defended. Beat Coupa by demonstrating working AP automation (vs failed Invoice Smash), unified Merlin AI platform (vs Coupa's bolt-on acquisitions), and active AI innovation (vs Thoma Bravo cost-cutting). Pricing must be comparable to Coupa (Elie's requirement), not cheaper, so win on superior fit and capability.

competitors.name: Unknown RFP shortlist vendors (2-3 others)
competitors.quote: We have one more vendor to meet next week, and then we will… down select to probably 4 vendors. (Elie Sauma, 15 May 2026)
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown competitors are the live peer threat in the RFP bake-off. Win by leading on the buyer's stated criteria: (1) unified AI platform with Merlin across intake/CLM/AP/sourcing, not fragmented point solutions; (2) Microsoft Teams intake and Azure compatibility (Sitecore is a strategic Microsoft partner); (3) proven Coupa-to-Zycus migration with Paul Hastings reference; (4) best-fit positioning (not cheapest) aligned to Elie's stated preference. Deliver a focused RFP response (due 30 June) on AP automation, vendor onboarding/OFAC, and AI capabilities, then secure a CFO business case co-authoring session to lock value alignment before the July decision.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Invoice smash has just not worked for us as a company... and Coupa themselves admitted it's not working... yet they would not give us credit back.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 9 Apr 2026 (Elie Sauma); SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Lead on AP Smartdesk automation, unified Merlin AI platform vs Coupa's bolted-on acquisitions, and Microsoft/Teams-native intake; match Coupa's current budget while proving migration is de-risked before renewal.

competitors.name: Unknown RFP shortlist competitors (3 others)
competitors.quote: Confirmed we will make the top 4 finalists. (Next Step 15 May).
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on AI depth (Merlin), unified platform vs point solutions, and Paul Hastings reference. After RFP submission (30 June), request a competitive readout from Elie to understand how Zycus scored vs the other 3 and adjust positioning before the CFO business case.

competitors.name: Unknown RFP Finalist 1
competitors.quote: We have one more vendor to meet next week, and then we will reconvene at the end of that week, kind of down select to probably 4 vendors.
competitors.how_we_win: Discover identity and positioning in early July check-in or follow-up demo conversation. Tailor follow-up demo and CFO business case to address what Sitecore sees as this competitor's strengths/weaknesses vs Zycus.

competitors.name: Unknown RFP Finalist 2
competitors.quote: We have one more vendor to meet next week, and then we will reconvene at the end of that week, kind of down select to probably 4 vendors.
competitors.how_we_win: Discover identity and positioning in early July check-in or follow-up demo conversation. Tailor follow-up demo and CFO business case to address what Sitecore sees as this competitor's strengths/weaknesses vs Zycus.

competitors.name: Unknown RFP Finalist 3
competitors.quote: We have one more vendor to meet next week, and then we will reconvene at the end of that week, kind of down select to probably 4 vendors.
competitors.how_we_win: Discover identity and positioning in early July check-in or follow-up demo conversation. Tailor follow-up demo and CFO business case to address what Sitecore sees as this competitor's strengths/weaknesses vs Zycus.

competitors.name: Unnamed RFP finalist 1
competitors.quote: I expect you guys to be definitely one of the 4 finalists.
competitors.how_we_win: Surgical RFP response on AP/finance use cases and OFAC/vendor onboarding; differentiate on AI fit and best-fit-within-budget criteria Elie stated.

competitors.name: Unnamed RFP finalist 2
competitors.quote: I expect you guys to be definitely one of the 4 finalists.
competitors.how_we_win: Win on demonstrated migration ease from Coupa and trusted Paul Hastings reference; lead the value case to the finance approver.

competitors.name: Unnamed RFP finalist 3
competitors.quote: I expect you guys to be definitely one of the 4 finalists.
competitors.how_we_win: Avoid a price-only comparison by embedding Zycus value (AI, unified platform) into the business case Elie takes to finance.

competitors.name: Relich
competitors.quote: We do have a third party tool that we use... we were looking at Relich, which integrates easily with Coupa... that is a part that we know is kind of a gap right now.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus native supplier-risk/ESG (anti-slavery, anti-bribery monitoring) as eliminating the need for a Relich-style bolt-on.

champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 9 Apr and 15 May 2026 (Elie Sauma)
champion_strength.summary: Elie Sauma is an active, transparent champion who brought the deal in via the trusted Paul Hastings reference and confirmed Zycus as a finalist. His advocacy is strong but he is procurement, not finance, and the deal is single-threaded to him; converting his advocacy into access to the finance approver and AP team is the next step.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The final budget approver has not been named, introduced, or engaged. Elie Sauma controls the procurement evaluation and is the decision driver, but on 15 May 2026 he made clear the commercial decision goes higher: 'I would like to be able to present back a business case in mid July latest... and get a decision by July', and earlier (9 April 2026) he referenced building the business case 'internally with our CFO because that... can take some time.' He also noted private-equity ownership tightened budgets ('they stepped in here a year ago. So things are super tight there'), signalling cost discipline at the approval level. So the evidence shows a finance approver exists above Elie and will receive the business case in mid-July, but that person is unmapped, unquoted, and unengaged. Identifying and getting a Zycus narrative in front of the economic buyer before the mid-July business case is the highest-value gap to close.

recommended_moves: Submit a tightly scoped RFP response leading on AP automation, OFAC and AI intake by EOD.
Ask Elie to name the finance approver and request a working session to co-build the business case.
Schedule the AP/finance follow-up demo on vendor onboarding, OFAC and AP workflows with the SVP of Accounting invited.
